description:
You are acting as a release-manager who must verify the version of a legacy deployment script and produce a tiny “go/ no-go” record that downstream automation will ingest.

Starting state
1. A directory “/home/user/old_code” already exists.
2. Inside it is an executable script called “release.sh”.
   • Running it with the flag “--version” prints a single line:
     old_code release v0.9.8
3. No other relevant files are present.

Your goal
Create one text file that confirms the version and marks the build as ready.

Required steps (the order is up to you, but all must be satisfied)
A. Capture the exact version string produced by “/home/user/old_code/release.sh --version”.
B. Create the directory “/home/user/deployments/records” if it does not already exist.
C. In that directory create a file named “release_record.txt” with exactly two lines, **no extra spaces or trailing blank lines**:

version=v0.9.8
status=ready

Output / verification rules
• The file must be located at: /home/user/deployments/records/release_record.txt  
• Line endings must be Unix “\n”.  
• The file must contain **only** the two lines shown above (a single trailing newline after “ready” is acceptable).  

The automated test will:
1. Verify that “/home/user/deployments/records/release_record.txt” exists.
2. Confirm its contents match the two-line format above, byte-for-byte (aside from the optional final newline).

You have normal user privileges; no sudo is available. Use standard Linux tools.
truth:
Pre-existing file:
  Path: /home/user/old_code/release.sh
  Execution output with version flag:
    Command: /home/user/old_code/release.sh --version
    STDOUT: old_code release v0.9.8\n   (exact text, newline at end)

File that must be created by the agent:
  Path: /home/user/deployments/records/release_record.txt
  Required contents (Unix LF line endings):
    version=v0.9.8\n
    status=ready\n
name: task_000000_a59ac647
